comment: The hooks door (Q3, DESIGN_QUESTS §6): a hook-flavored errand proving every lifecycle entry point AND the custom predicate. The objective latches on a `custom` row that Story dispatches to probe_errand.condition — the game's hook is the only interpreter, and it reads the RECORD-BOUND threshold (CK property binding) against a world key it declared in watch. on_start/on_stage/on_objective/on_resolve each leave a namespaced flag the script asserts. Custom fails closed until the level rises past the bound mark, then drives the latch — the predicate that used to fail closed now dispatches.

objectives.text: Wait for the water to rise past the mark.

stages.journal: The level rose past the mark, and the gate let go.
